texto: I hand over a lot of things when I'm home. Mom tells me she doesn't like my shirt I want to buy, and I hand it over. Not the shirt itself, but my wish for that shirt. I want to watch one television show and she wants to watch another one -I hand that over too. It's easier that way. I even hand over my toenails when she asks. But I think sometimes you need to put a thing in a box -even if the box is inside your head -and store it away for yourself.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Gin Phillips: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “I hand over a lot of things when I'm home. Mom tells me she doesn't like my shirt I want to buy, …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, insightful, mentality pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de mentality e moti.

But I think sometimes you need to put a thing in a box -even if the box is inside your head -and store it away for yourself.
